contacts.name: Customer Service
contacts.hours: Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
contacts.purpose: General inquiries, complaints, compliments, account questions, eligibility, card replacement, coupon orders

contacts.name: Reservations
contacts.hours: 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, 7 days a week
contacts.purpose: Book trips, modify reservations, cancel trips, request standing orders, check on trips
contacts.note: Select your region after calling, then choose option for your need

contacts.name: TDD Customer Service
contacts.hours: Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
contacts.purpose: Customer service for deaf and hard-of-hearing callers using TDD/TTY devices

contacts.name: TDD Reservations
contacts.hours: 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, 7 days a week
contacts.purpose: Trip reservations for deaf and hard-of-hearing callers using TDD/TTY devices

contacts.name: Operations Monitoring Center (OMC)
contacts.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, including holidays
contacts.purpose: Real-time trip issues: late vehicles, missed connections, ETAs, same-day emergencies, serious service disruptions
capabilities: Request Estimated Time of Arrival (available 15 minutes before pickup)
Serious service disruptions requiring immediate response
Secure alternate Access vehicle or backup provider

contacts.name: Trip Status via Text
contacts.method: Text the word 'TRIP' to this number
contacts.purpose: Check trip status, get ETA updates, report missed trips via SMS
contacts.note: Standard text messaging rates apply. Messages answered in order received.

contacts.name: Where's My Ride Hotline
contacts.purpose: Help with the Where's My Ride app and vehicle tracking

contacts.name: Online Reservations Support
contacts.purpose: Help with online booking system, Rider360 portal, technical issues

contacts.name: Eligibility Evaluation Scheduling
contacts.purpose: Schedule your in-person transit evaluation after receiving your Access ID number
contacts.note: Call 3 days after online application or 7 days after mailing application

contacts.name: Reasonable Modification Coordinator
contacts.purpose: Request reasonable modifications to Access policies and practices to accommodate disability
contacts.mail: Access Services, ATTN: RMC, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734

contacts.name: ADA Coordinator
contacts.purpose: ADA compliance issues, nondiscrimination inquiries, formal ADA complaints, Title VI complaints
contacts.mail: Access Services, Customer Relations ADA Coordinator, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734

contacts.name: General Information / Main Line
contacts.purpose: General agency inquiries, department connections

contacts.name: InfoLine
contacts.purpose: Service announcements, meeting schedule updates, general information

contacts.name: Fax
contacts.purpose: Send documents, applications, visitor documentation

contacts.name: Travel Training (Mobility Management Partners)
contacts.purpose: Free one-on-one travel training to learn fixed-route public transit
contacts.note: Established 1991, providing travel training in California since 1994

contacts.name: Ride Info / Transit Options
contacts.hours: Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
contacts.purpose: Local transit service referrals, transit options counseling

contacts.name: Procurement and Contracts
contacts.purpose: Vendor inquiries, RFPs, procurement opportunities, contract administration

contacts.name: Community Advisory Committee Applications
contacts.mail: Access Services, Attn: Community Advisory Committee, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734
contacts.purpose: Apply to serve on the Community Advisory Committee

contacts.name: Parents with Disabilities Program
contacts.purpose: Enroll in the Parents with Disabilities Program for school trips
contacts.mail: Access Services, Parents with Disabilities Program, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734-1728

contacts.name: Metro (LA County Metropolitan Transportation Authority)
contacts.purpose: Fixed-route bus and rail information, TAP card, trip planning

contacts.name: Personal Assistance Services Council (PASC)
contacts.purpose: Help finding personal care aides for riders who need assistance beyond what drivers provide

contacts.name: Federal Transit Administration Civil Rights
contacts.mail: Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights, Attention: Complaint Team, East Building, 5th Floor TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave SE, Washington, DC 20590
contacts.purpose: Federal civil rights complaints about transit service discrimination

contacts.name: 211 Information Line
contacts.purpose: Connect with local independent living centers or regional centers for advocacy assistance
